channel.title: +अधि-चान्द्र-नक्षत्रम् on ज्योतिषे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +अधि-चान्द्र-नक्षत्रम् on ज्योतिषे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.title: ०१ अयनचलनानुसारम् परिवर्तनम्
item.description: पुरा तारा-मण्डल-सङ्गतान्यासन् नक्षत्राणि (lunar mansions [sky regions] coincided with the eponymous asterisms/ stars)। अधुना नक्षत्राणि तारमण्डलेभ्यश् चलितानि (अयनचलनानुसारं ऋतुप्रारम्भचलम् भवति, तच् चोचितनक्षत्र एव स्याद् इति व्यवहारसौकर्यार्थम् प्रयत्नः)। तेषाम् मण्डलानां सूचिश् च पाश्चात्य-नाम-पर्यायो ऽत्र। eclpitic longitude इति द्रष्टव्यम्। Saha Lahiri report IMG. YV.

item.title: ०४ अयनांशचयनम्
item.description: The ayanAMsha used to determine nakShatra or lunar month in a particular ritual context should be determined from the vidhi-vAkya-s enjoining that particular ritual action. Apastamba example Take upAkarma. Apastamba says: “(आर्तव)श्रवणा(पूर्व)पक्ष ओषधीषु जातासु हस्तेन पौर्णमास्यां वा ऽध्यायोपाकर्म २”. So, when do saplings sprout? At the beginning of the (khArif) rainy season, around the summer solstice time in north India. This happened to be shrAvaNa around 1000BCE. The crops don’t srout when xyz-ayanAMsha based month happens to be “shrAvaNa”.

item.title: ०५ अयनांशाः
item.description: Intro to sidereal positioning “There are also sidereal traditions of astrology, both a Hindu tradition and a western tradition, which derives itself from ancient Hellenistic and Babylonian astrology. They use a so-called sidereal zodiac, which consists of 12 equal-sized zodiac signs, too, but it is tied to some fixed reference point, i.e. usually some fixed star. These sidereal zodiac signs only roughly coincide with the sidereal zodiacal constellations, which are of variable size.

item.title: ०६ नक्षत्रपुरुषः
item.description: उत्तरनारायणः पुरुषो वा यस्य “नक्षत्राणि रूपम्, अश्विनौ व्यात्तम्” (MT, आरण्यके ऽत्र) " This tradition of stellar puruSha continued down to the purANa-s (e.g agnipurANa chapter 196) and is also specified in detail in the 105th chapter of the bR^ihat-saMhitA of the great naturalist varAhamihira. In this later version the nakShatra puruSha is also invoked as viShNu (shAmbhavAyanIya ritual) during every month of the year. …In the parallel later bAdarAyaNIya ritual, the stellar prajApati is comprised of rAshI-s instead of nakShatra-s.
